prose:
Fukui reactivity indices computed for formaldehyde (CH₂O) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Exact four-center integrals (no density fitting). Geometry taken as-is from the user-supplied formaldehyde.xyz; no re-optimization at this level. Partial charges via Mulliken (PySCF). N−1 (cation): charge +1, multiplicity 2; N+1 (anion): charge −1, multiplicity 2.

Fukui indices per atom (O, C, H, H):
  f⁺: [0.46814, 0.70839, −0.08827, −0.08826] — sum = 1.0000 ✅
  f⁻: [0.46742, 0.11244,  0.21008,  0.21006] — sum = 1.0000 ✅
  f⁰: [0.46778, 0.41042,  0.06090,  0.06090]
  Dual (f⁺−f⁻): [+0.00071, +0.59595, −0.29835, −0.29832]

Headline value reported: f⁺(C) = 0.70839 — the carbon atom (atom 2) is the most electrophilic site (most susceptible to nucleophilic attack), consistent with formaldehyde's well-known carbonyl reactivity. The oxygen (atom 1) is the most nucleophilic site with f⁻ = 0.46742.

Integrity: trustworthy = true, status = ok. No engine warnings reported.
